title: A Lightweight Deep Network for Efficient CSI Feedback in Massive MIMO Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight deep CSI feedback network for massive MIMO with explicit focus on user-equipment deployment cost. It redesigns the encoder-decoder pipeline to reduce encoder complexity while preserving reconstruction quality at low feedback rates.
key_contributions: Introduces a lightweight CSI feedback network tailored for resource-constrained UE inference.
Reduces encoder-side complexity through compact convolutional design while keeping decoder-side recovery capability strong.
Demonstrates improved complexity-accuracy trade-offs against prior CSI feedback baselines in low-rate settings.
Provides deployment-oriented analysis for practical massive MIMO feedback pipelines.
algorithms: Transform channel matrix to angle-delay representation and normalize input.
Use lightweight convolutional encoder blocks to produce compact feedback codewords.
Transmit fixed-length codewords over the feedback link.
Apply stronger decoder refinement blocks at BS to recover high-fidelity CSI.
Train end-to-end with NMSE-driven objective and deployment-aware complexity tracking.

key_equations.name: Encoder Complexity (Convolutional FLOPs)
key_equations.latex: C_{\mathrm{enc}} = \sum_{l=1}^{L} H_l W_l K_l^2 C_{\mathrm{in},l} C_{\mathrm{out},l}
key_equations.description: Layer-wise encoder computation cost. Lightweight design focuses on lowering this sum for UE-side inference.

key_equations.name: Depthwise-Separable Approximation
key_equations.latex: C_{\mathrm{dw+pw}} = \sum_{l=1}^{L} H_l W_l\left(K_l^2 C_{\mathrm{in},l} + C_{\mathrm{in},l} C_{\mathrm{out},l}\right)
key_equations.description: A compact factorization view that highlights complexity savings over standard convolution blocks.

key_equations.name: NMSE Objective
key_equations.description: Primary reconstruction objective used to compare feedback quality under fixed rate constraints.

notes_summary: A deployment-oriented lightweight CSI feedback model that targets UE compute constraints while maintaining reconstruction quality.
architecture_detail:
## 1) Problem Setup
UE-side CSI feedback models must run under strict latency, memory, and energy budgets. The paper treats complexity as a first-class design objective, not just an implementation afterthought.

## 2) Design Goal
The target is to minimize encoder compute while preserving reconstruction fidelity at BS. This creates a practical asymmetric architecture where UE encoder is compact and BS decoder handles heavier recovery.

## 3) Lightweight Encoder Strategy
The encoder uses compact convolutional blocks and narrow channel dimensions to reduce FLOPs and parameter count. The design can be interpreted via [Depthwise Separable Convolution](/glossary?q=Depthwise%20Separable%20Convolution) style factorization and low-cost projection layers.

## 4) Decoder-Side Compensation
Decoder blocks are allowed to be relatively stronger because BS resources are less constrained. This asymmetry keeps end-to-end NMSE competitive under strict feedback budget.

## 5) Complexity-NMSE Trade-off
The practical objective is not only lower NMSE but lower NMSE *at a fixed UE compute envelope*. The model is evaluated on both reconstruction accuracy and deployment efficiency.

## 6) Key Training/Optimization Notes
- End-to-end training with NMSE objective.
- Monitor encoder FLOPs and parameter count alongside validation NMSE.
- Keep feedback codeword length fixed to align with protocol constraints.

## 7) Why This Paper Matters
This work systematically pushes CSI feedback architecture toward deployable UE models, complementing later quantization/bit-allocation studies that optimize transmission cost on top of lightweight networks.
